mechanism_narrative: A sustained decline in the federal funds rate reduces the discount rate applied to future corporate earnings, directly compressing equity valuation multiples and generating positive S&P 500 forward returns over the subsequent month.
failure_condition: Walk-forward Rank IC remains below 0.03 over any 2-year rolling window, or 20-day forward returns in easing periods are not significantly higher than baseline (t-test p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: A monthly drop in the unemployment rate below 5.5 percent signals labor market tightness that supports consumer spending and corporate revenue growth, driving positive S&P 500 forward returns over a one-month horizon.
failure_condition: The signal produces negative or flat forward returns in more than 60 percent of triggered instances, or the cross-sectional predictive rank IC drops below 0.02 over a 3-year window.

mechanism_narrative: Rising housing starts combined with increasing residential construction financing indicate a synchronized demand shock that boosts construction-related equities and consumer confidence, lifting S&P 500 returns over a quarter.
failure_condition: Forward returns are statistically indistinguishable from zero when both series rise simultaneously, or the joint signal yields a negative Sharpe ratio over a 5-year backtest.

mechanism_narrative: Industrial production growth exceeding 0.5 percent month-over-month reflects supply-side capacity utilization that outpaces demand, triggering margin compression and negative S&P 500 forward returns over a one-week horizon.
failure_condition: The indicator consistently predicts positive returns during supply booms, or the one-week forward return distribution shows no significant skew toward negative outcomes.

mechanism_narrative: Quarterly real GDP growth above 2.0 percent annualized confirms underlying economic momentum that sustains corporate earnings revisions upward, producing positive S&P 500 forward returns over a monthly horizon.
failure_condition: Quarterly GDP beats coincide with equity drawdowns in more than 50 percent of observations, or the predictive information ratio falls below 0.1 over a rolling 4-year window.

mechanism_narrative: A month-over-month increase in the consumer price index exceeding 0.4 percent signals accelerating inflation that forces the Federal Reserve to tighten policy, raising discount rates and depressing equity valuations over a week.
failure_condition: Equity markets rally persistently despite CPI prints above the threshold, or the 5-day forward return median remains positive across all inflation acceleration episodes.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous growth in total nonfarm payrolls and a declining unemployment rate confirms a self-reinforcing labor market recovery that drives consumer spending and equity risk premiums higher over a quarterly horizon.
failure_condition: The joint signal fails to outperform a buy-and-hold benchmark over a 3-year period, or forward returns are flat when payroll gains exceed 200,000 and unemployment falls simultaneously.

mechanism_narrative: When real GDP growth accelerates alongside a contraction in the unemployment rate, the resulting demand-supply imbalance forces price increases that compress real margins, generating negative S&P 500 forward returns over a month.
candidate_trigger: GDPC1 growth > 2.5% annualized AND UNRATE falls > 0.2pp
failure_condition: Forward returns remain positive when both GDP accelerates and unemployment drops, or the interaction term shows no statistical significance in a multivariate regression over a 5-year window.
